text.div: intentionally left empty / not meaningful

tr.td: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Human isophane insulin 70 unit/mL and recombinant human regular insulin 30 unit/mL solution for injection
Inject 10 units (0.1 mL) subcutaneously before breakfast and 10 units (0.1 mL) before dinner
1 tablet (25 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
Albuterol (as albuterol sulfate) 830 microgram/mL solution for inhalation
2.5 mg (3 mL of 830 microgram/mL solution) via nebulizer every 6 hours as needed
Alendronic acid (as alendronate sodium) 10 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (50 mg) by mouth once daily
Clopidogrel (as clopidogrel bisulfate) 75 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (75 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (20 mg) by mouth once daily
400 micrograms (1 spray) under the tongue as needed for chest pain, may repeat every 5 minutes as needed, up to 3 sprays in 15 minutes
Metoprolol tartrate (as metoprolol succinate) 100 mg prolonged-release oral tablet
Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product
Assessment of health and social care needs
Assessment using AUDIT-C (Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test - Consumption)
Depression screening using PHQ-9 (Patient Health Questionnaire 9) score

ul.li: Patient will monitor and record weight daily to detect early signs of fluid retention.
Patient will adhere to a sodium-restricted diet as recommended.
Patient will limit daily fluid intake as recommended.
Patient will maintain blood pressure within the individualized target range.
Patient will recognize and promptly report symptoms of heart failure exacerbation such as increased shortness of breath, swelling, or rapid weight gain.
Achieve and maintain a healthy body weight to reduce risk of progression from prediabetes to diabetes
Maintain HbA1c below diabetes diagnostic threshold
Maintain blood pressure within recommended range for cardiovascular and renal protection
Engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity physical activity per week
Follow a balanced diet with reduced simple sugars and saturated fats to support glycemic control and cardiovascular health
Reduce urinary albumin excretion to slow progression of diabetic kidney disease
Achieve and maintain systolic blood pressure below 130 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Achieve and maintain diastolic blood pressure below 80 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Patient will demonstrate effective self-management of asthma symptoms, including correct inhaler technique and adherence to prescribed therapy.

tr.td: Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported
Blood pressure panel with all children optional
Urea nitrogen [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma

Glucose [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

Bilirubin.total [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

Ketones [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

pH of Urine by Test strip

Protein [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Nitrite [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Hemoglobin [Presence] in Urine by Test strip
Leukocyte esterase [Presence] in Urine by Test strip

ul.li: Patient will monitor and record weight daily to detect early signs of fluid retention.
Patient will adhere to a sodium-restricted diet as recommended.
Patient will limit daily fluid intake as recommended.
Patient will maintain blood pressure within the individualized target range.
Patient will recognize and promptly report symptoms of heart failure exacerbation such as increased shortness of breath, swelling, or rapid weight gain.

ul.li: Achieve and maintain a healthy body weight to reduce risk of progression from prediabetes to diabetes
Maintain HbA1c below diabetes diagnostic threshold
Maintain blood pressure within recommended range for cardiovascular and renal protection
Engage in at least 150 minutes of moderate-intensity physical activity per week
Follow a balanced diet with reduced simple sugars and saturated fats to support glycemic control and cardiovascular health
Reduce urinary albumin excretion to slow progression of diabetic kidney disease
Achieve and maintain systolic blood pressure below 130 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Achieve and maintain diastolic blood pressure below 80 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Patient will demonstrate effective self-management of asthma symptoms, including correct inhaler technique and adherence to prescribed therapy.
